requests.title: My Requests

hint.wfh: Use this form when you plan to work from home. Your manager will review and approve your request. Approved WFH days appear on the team calendar so everyone knows your location.
hint.off: Use this form to request time off (annual leave, sick leave, etc.). Select the leave type, date range, and the number of hours to deduct from your leave balance.
hint.equipment: Use this form to request equipment (laptop, monitor, keyboard, etc.) from the company. Specify the item, quantity, and where it will be used.
hint.clock_forget: Use this form if you forgot to clock in or out on a specific day. Your manager will verify and update your attendance record accordingly.
hint.overtime: Use this form to report and get approval for overtime hours after you have already worked them. Enter the actual hours worked for your manager to confirm.
hint.business_trip: Use this form when you need to travel for work. During an approved business trip, you can clock in/out from anywhere, not just the office network.

requests.ccHint: CC people who need to stay informed — e.g. your team lead, a colleague covering your work, or anyone affected by this request.
requests.ccGroupsHint: CC an entire group — all members will be notified. Useful when a whole team needs to know.
requests.unitHoursHint: Auto-calculated: Mon–Fri, 08:00–17:00, excl. lunch 12:00–13:00. You can override manually.
requests.totalHoursOtHint: Auto-calculated: total elapsed time minus regular working hours (Mon–Fri 08:00–17:00). You can override manually.

howItWorks.title: How Attendance Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Overview of all attendance methods and data flow

prerequisites.title: Before You Start
prerequisites.subtitle: These conditions must be met before any attendance method can work.
prerequisites.employeeDepartment: Employee must belong to a department
prerequisites.employeeDepartmentDesc: The system uses department membership to determine the employee's company. Go to Users → assign a department.
prerequisites.companyConfiguredDesc: Company name, settings, and IP whitelist (for QR clock) must be configured in Company Settings.
prerequisites.userAccount: Employee must have a system account
prerequisites.userAccountDesc: Every employee who clocks attendance must have a user account with the correct email and role.

zkDevice.title: ZKTeco Biometric Device
zkDevice.subtitle: Fingerprint scanner at the entrance
zkDevice.description: Employees clock in and out by scanning their fingerprint on a ZKTeco device. The zk-relay agent running on a local machine pulls records from the device via TCP and pushes them to the system automatically.
zkDevice.step1Desc: The ZKTeco device records the punch with a timestamp.
zkDevice.step2Desc: A Node.js relay agent runs on a local PC, connects to the device via TCP every few minutes, and fetches new records.
zkDevice.step3Desc: The relay agent converts records to ATTLOG format and POSTs to /iclock endpoint. Earliest punch = clock-in, latest punch = clock-out.
zkDevice.step4Desc: The system matches the device user ID to an employee and creates the attendance record.
zkDevice.note: The zk-relay agent must be running on a machine that has network access to the ZKTeco device. Configure the device IP and API endpoint in config.js.
zkDevice.req1: Employee's Device User ID must be set in their user profile (Users page → Device User ID field)
zkDevice.req2: zk-relay agent must be running and configured with the correct device IP and API URL
zkDevice.req3: Network connectivity between the relay PC and the ZKTeco device

qrClock.title: QR Code Clock
qrClock.subtitle: Admin displays QR, employees scan with phone
qrClock.description: An admin displays the daily QR code (visible on the Home page). Employees scan it with their phone browser. The QR is valid for the current day only and refreshes automatically at midnight.
qrClock.step1Desc: The QR widget on the Home page shows today's QR code automatically.
qrClock.step2Desc: Phone must be on the office Wi-Fi (IP whitelist enforced). The browser opens /clock?token=...
qrClock.step3Desc: First scan of the day = clock-in. Second scan = clock-out. Result displayed immediately.
qrClock.note: IP whitelist is required — configure allowed IP ranges in Company Settings to prevent clocking from outside the office.
qrClock.req1: Company must have at least one IP range configured in Company Settings → Allowed IPs
qrClock.req2: Employee's phone must be connected to a network whose IP matches the whitelist
qrClock.req3: Employee must be logged in to the system on their phone browser

appClock.title: App Clock (Home page)
appClock.subtitle: Direct clock-in / clock-out button in the app
appClock.description: Employees can clock in and out directly from the Home page using the Clock In / Clock Out buttons. No device or QR needed.
appClock.step1Desc: The current status (clocked in / not clocked in) is shown in the Today panel.
appClock.step2Title: Click Clock In or Clock Out
appClock.step2Desc: The button sends a request to the API with the current timestamp.
appClock.step3Desc: The record is immediately visible in Attendance Logs.
appClock.note: App clock is only available for employees with an approved WFH (Work From Home) request for today. Office employees use ZK device or QR clock.
appClock.req1: Employee must have an approved WFH request for today
appClock.req2: Employee must be logged in to the system

faceCheckin.title: Face Recognition Checkin
faceCheckin.subtitle: Look at a camera to clock in — two modes depending on the device
faceCheckin.description: There are two ways to use face checkin depending on who is logged in on the device. On a personal device (logged in as a regular employee), only that employee can clock themselves in or out. On a shared kiosk device (logged in as an admin), any employee can walk up and be recognized. This prevents employees from accidentally or intentionally clocking out a colleague early.
faceCheckin.step1Title: Open face checkin on a device
faceCheckin.step1Desc: Personal device (logged in as employee): only that employee can be clocked. Admin device: acts as a shared station — any employee can use it.
faceCheckin.step2Desc: The system captures a live image and matches it against all KYC-approved employees. If the matched face belongs to someone other than the logged-in user and the device is not an admin account, the request is rejected.
faceCheckin.step3Title: Attendance recorded for the recognized employee
faceCheckin.step3Desc: Clock-in or clock-out is recorded. The captured image is saved with the log for audit purposes.
faceCheckin.req1: Employee's KYC must be approved — employee submits face photo from their profile, admin verifies
faceCheckin.req2: Personal device: logged in as the employee being clocked. Shared kiosk: logged in as an admin account
faceCheckin.req3: Device must be on the company's allowed IP list, OR employee must have an approved WFH request or permanent remote status
faceCheckin.req4: Good lighting at the checkin point

kyc.title: KYC — Face Enrollment Verification
kyc.subtitle: Employee submits face photo → admin approves → face checkin enabled
kyc.distinction: KYC is NOT a clock-in method. It is the face enrollment process that enables face checkin. Each employee submits their face from their profile page. Once admin approves, that employee's face can be recognized at any face checkin kiosk.
kyc.description: Before using the face checkin kiosk, each employee must complete KYC: submit a face photo from their profile page. The system extracts a face descriptor and sets KYC status to 'pending'. Admins review the submission and approve or reject. Only KYC-approved employees can be matched by the face checkin system.
kyc.step1Title: Employee submits face photo from their profile
kyc.step1Desc: Employee opens their profile, takes a face photo using the device camera. The system extracts the face descriptor and sets KYC status to 'pending'. Admins are notified automatically.
kyc.step2Title: Admin reviews in KYC Approval page
kyc.step2Desc: Admin opens the KYC Approval page, reviews the submitted face photo, and approves or rejects. If rejected, a reason is recorded and the employee must re-submit.
kyc.step3Desc: Once approved, the employee's face descriptor is stored and marked as verified. The employee can now clock in at any face checkin kiosk without any manual action.

dataStorage.title: Where Data Is Stored
dataStorage.description: All attendance records from every method are stored in the same Attendance Logs table. Each record contains:

logic.title: Business Rules
logic.clockInDesc: The earliest record of the day is treated as the clock-in time.
logic.clockOutDesc: The latest record of the day is treated as the clock-out time.
logic.forgotClockDesc: Employees can submit a Forgot Clock request for admin approval to manually correct the record.
logic.ipWhitelistDesc: QR clock-in is restricted to devices on allowed IP ranges. Configure in Company Settings.

qrClock.title: QR Clock Code
qrClock.scanInstruction: Employees scan this QR code to clock in/out
qrClock.invalidQrDesc: Missing token, company, or date in URL.
